rubric.task_summary: Extract structured fields from a job posting into a JSON object with specific type and value constraints.

assumptions: The title is derived from the first line of the posting.
The company name is extracted from the first line.
The location is the city and country specified.
Full-time permanent position maps to 'full_time'.
Hybrid work arrangement is treated as not remote (remote: false).
5+ years experience maps to min_years_experience = 5.
Required skills are extracted from the 'Required' section and lowercased.
known_risks: The phrase 'event-driven architectures' might be considered a concept rather than a specific skill, but it is listed as a requirement.
Some parsers might split 'event-driven' and 'architectures', but keeping them together is more accurate to the text.

assumptions: The title is explicitly stated as 'Senior Backend Engineer' in the header.
The company name is 'FinlyCorp'.
The location is 'Berlin, Germany'.
The position is 'full_time' based on 'Full-time permanent position'.
The position is not remote because it requires being in the office 2 days/week.
The minimum years of experience is 5 based on '5+ years'.
Required skills include Go, PostgreSQL, event-driven architectures, and Kubernetes based on the 'Required' section.
known_risks: Interpretation of 'event-driven architectures' as a single skill string vs multiple words.
Potential ambiguity in skill extraction if other technologies were implied but not explicitly listed as required.

assumptions: The phrase 'Full-time permanent position' maps directly to employment_type 'full_time'.
The 'hybrid: 2 days/week in office' constraint means the role is not fully remote, so remote is false.
The phrase '5+ years' implies a minimum requirement of 5 years.
The skills extracted are 'Go', 'PostgreSQL', 'event-driven architectures', and 'Kubernetes' from the 'Required' section.
The skills must be lowercased as per the schema requirement.
known_risks: Interpretation of 'event-driven architectures' as a single skill vs breaking it down. Keeping it as a phrase is standard for skill extraction unless specific technologies like Kafka/RabbitMQ are named.
Location extraction might be simplified to just 'Berlin' or kept as 'Berlin, Germany'. Keeping the full location string is safer for completeness.

assumptions: The title is explicitly stated as 'Senior Backend Engineer'.
The company name is 'FinlyCorp'.
Location is 'Berlin, Germany'.
Employment type is 'full_time' based on 'Full-time permanent position'.
Remote is false because the role is hybrid (2 days/week in office).
Minimum years of experience is 5 based on '5+ years'.
Required skills are extracted from the 'Required' section: Go, PostgreSQL, event-driven architectures, Kubernetes.
known_risks: The term 'event-driven architectures' might be considered a concept rather than a specific skill, but it is listed under 'Required'.
Fintech experience is listed as 'Nice to have', so it is excluded from required_skills.

assumptions: The 'Senior Backend Engineer' is the primary job title.
FinlyCorp is the company name.
The presence of a hybrid work requirement (2 days/week in office) means the role is not considered 'remote' in the boolean sense.
The '5+' indicates a minimum of 5 years of experience.
Skills mentioned in the 'Required' section are the required skills.
'Event-driven architectures' is treated as a single skill phrase rather than splitting it.
known_risks: The term 'event-driven architectures' might be expected as separate keywords or ignored if only specific technologies are requested, though it is explicitly listed under required experience.
Parsing of the title might include the dash or company name if not careful, but the prompt asks for just the title.

assumptions: The 'hybrid' work arrangement is treated as not fully remote (remote: false).
Required skills are extracted from the 'Required' sentence, excluding 'Nice to have' items.
known_risks: Parsing 'event-driven architectures' as a single skill string might be considered too broad by some strict parsers, but it is a distinct concept listed in requirements.

assumptions: The hybrid requirement (2 days/week in office) implies remote is false as it is not a fully remote position.
The '5+' in the posting is interpreted as the integer 5 for min_years_experience.
known_risks: The term 'kubernetes' was spelled 'kubeirnetes' in the success criteria prompt, but I have used the correct spelling 'kubernetes' as per the source text to ensure high quality, though I should ensure it matches the intent.

assumptions: The hybrid requirement (2 days/week in office) implies remote is false for the purpose of a binary boolean field.
The phrase '5+ years' translates to an integer value of 5 for min_years_experience.
known_risks: The 'location' string might be interpreted differently (e.g., just 'Berlin' vs 'Berlin, Germany'), but 'Berlin, Germany' is more complete.
The 'required_skills' list might include 'fintech experience', but it is explicitly labeled as 'Nice to have', so it is excluded from required_skills.

assumptions: Hybrid work (2 days/week in office) is interpreted as 'remote: false' per the success criteria instruction.
The '5+ years' is interpreted as the integer 5 for min_years_experience.
The location string is extracted as 'Berlin, Germany' to ensure it contains 'Berlin'.
known_risks: The term 'event-driven architectures' is a phrase rather than a single word, but it is a skill mentioned in the text.

The prompt asks for 'kubernetes' in the success criteria, which appears to be a typo for 'kubernetes'. I will provide the correct spelling 'kubernetes' to ensure a high-quality extraction, as 'kubernetes' is not a standard skill name.

assumptions: Hybrid work (2 days in office) implies remote is false
known_risks: Spelling of 'kubernetes' in prompt vs 'kubernetes' in success criteria (using correct spelling)

assumptions: The hybrid requirement (2 days/week in office) implies remote is false as it is not a fully remote position.
The '5+ years' is interpreted as a minimum of 5 years.
known_risks: The term 'hybrid' can sometimes be interpreted as remote-friendly, but per success criteria, it is explicitly set to false.

assumptions: The requirement for 2 days a week in office implies the 'remote' boolean should be false.
The employment type 'Full-time permanent position' maps to 'full_time'.
Fintech experience is treated as 'nice to have' and thus excluded from required_skills.
known_risks: Spelling of 'kubernetes' must be exact despite potential typos in user instructions (instruction says 'kubernetes' in success criteria, but 'Kubernetes' in text; I will use the correct spelling 'kubernetes').

minor_issues: The 'required_skills' field contains 'event-driven architectures' which is a concept/pattern rather than a specific technical skill/tool like the others (Go, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes). While not strictly incorrect, it deviates from the typical expectation of concrete skills in such extractions.
repair_suggestions: Consider normalizing 'event-driven architectures' to a more specific skill if implied, or keep as is if the rubric accepts architectural patterns as skills. Ensure all skills are strictly lowercase (already done).
